description: §48.2.1 — the `fn(args) => expr` form (the `fn` keyword + a parenthesized param list + an ARROW `=>` body) is NOT a sanctioned scrml shape and SHALL fire E-FN-ARROW-BODY at parse. `arr.map(fn(n) => n * 2)` mixes the two canonical anonymous-callable forms — the block-body anonymous `fn` (`fn(args) { return expr }`) and the plain inline lambda (`args => expr`). Reject-path (pos) half of the pin; the clean neg is `fn/plain-arrow-clean` (the valid `n => n * 2` inline lambda — silent). Regression guard: pre-fix this fell through the codegen `fn`→`function` text-rewrite to invalid JS, mis-framed as E-CODEGEN-INVALID-LOGIC.
rationale: §48.2.1: the two canonical anonymous-callable forms are the block-body anonymous `fn` (`fn(args) { return expr }`) and the plain inline lambda (`args => expr`). The mixed `fn(args) => expr` form is unsanctioned and is rejected at parse with E-FN-ARROW-BODY, which steers to both valid forms.
